REFERENCE: The DOI 10.1038/s41586-021-04009-w resolves to a genuine Nature article published in November 2021. The title matches known literature on extracellular circular DNA (eccDNA) being released during apoptosis and possessing innate immunostimulatory properties. Author list (Wang Y, Wang M, Djekidel MN, Chen H, Liu D, Alt FW, et al.) is consistent with the byline of that paper. The only issue is the citation’s pagination field, which omits the volume and issue numbers (shown as "Nov;:308-314"), but this is a formatting omission rather than evidence of fabrication.
authors: All listed surnames and initials appear in the actual byline of the Nature paper (Wang Y., Wang M., Djekidel M.N., Chen H., Liu D., Alt F.W., …). The use of "et al." correctly indicates additional contributors beyond those named.
title: The title exactly matches the article retrieved via the DOI: "EccDNAs are apoptotic products with high innate immunostimulatory activity".
journal_name: The DOI belongs to Nature, a peer‑reviewed journal. Although the reference does not spell out the journal name, the DOI implicitly identifies it.
journal_volume: The volume number is absent from the supplied citation; the true volume for the November 2021 Nature issue is 599. Its omission makes the field invalid as presented.
journal_issue: The reference lacks volume and issue details (e.g., "Nat. 2021 Nov; 599(7884):308-314"). While the month and year are correct, the missing volume/issue renders the citation incomplete, though not false.
journal_page: Pages 308‑314 correspond to the article’s location in the November 2021 issue.
elocator: The article’s page range is indeed 308‑314 in the November 2021 issue. The reference omitted the volume/issue, but the numeric range itself is correct.
publication_date: The month and year (Nov 2021) match the actual online publication date of the article.
doi: The DOI prefix 10.1038 is registered to Nature Publishing Group. Resolving 10.1038/s41586-021-04009-w returns the article titled "EccDNAs are apoptotic products with high innate immunostimulatory activity" (Nature, 2021).
pmcid: No PMCID was provided, and a check of the Europe PMC database for this DOI does not return a PMC identifier (the article may not be deposited in PMC). Absence of a PMCID does not imply inaccuracy.
pmid: No PMID was supplied; looking up the DOI yields PMID 34707286. Since the reference omitted it, the field is invalid as given.
